2025-04-18.thesis: China imposes rare-earth export curbs, MP sole scaled US producer
2025-04-18.exit_case: China's export curbs already announced and publicized; MP already surged pre/at-entry — initial price move may be exhausted.
2025-04-18.assessment: Week 1. The catalyst — China's rare-earth export curbs — is confirmed active and unresolved. Curbs were imposed ~April 4-7; MP surged into entry. Critically, the restrictions have NOT been reversed: broader US-China trade hostilities deepened this week (China halting Boeing deliveries, EU tariff talks collapsing), making a near-term RE restriction rollback unlikely. MP remains the sole scaled US rare-earth producer, uniquely positioned for the ongoing supply disruption. The 'already surged' risk is real (exit_case above is genuine), but the ongoing nature of the restriction — not a one-time announcement — means the catalyst is still live. Watch for: (1) any China-US framework deal covering RE; (2) MP management commentary on offtake/pricing uplift. Vehicle unchanged: MP is the purest single play.
2025-04-18.exit_advice: Exit if China formally lifts or materially relaxes rare-earth export restrictions, or if a US-China trade deal explicitly restores RE supply chains.

2025-04-18.thesis: Selected to bid for US/UK government launch contracts worth billions
2025-04-18.exit_case: Selected-to-bid announcement already public and priced — stock surged Apr 14-15 before entry date; catalyst is backward-looking.
2025-04-18.assessment: First week. The 'selected to bid' announcement broke April 14-15 and drove a visible gap-up in RKLB — that move is already in the price. The devil's-advocate case is real: the triggering news is public and the stock has already reacted. However, being placed on a government-approved vendor shortlist is an intermediate milestone (analogous to FDA acceptance of an NDA for review), NOT the resolution. The billions in actual contract value have not been awarded to anyone. The thesis lives on the pending contract-award decisions from DoD/USAF and UK MOD — those decisions represent the genuine price-discovery moment. RKLB remains the only pure-play vehicle for this specific event. Watch for: formal RFP releases, contract-award announcements, and any signal competitors (ULA, SpaceX via exemption, ABL) are preferred. The primary risk near-term is time decay — if the procurement timeline slips 12+ months, the position loses its event-driven character.
2025-04-18.exit_advice: Hold while US/UK contract awards remain pending; exit immediately if contracts are formally awarded to competitors or the procurement process is cancelled or restructured to exclude RKLB.

2025-05-16.thesis: China imposes rare-earth export curbs, MP sole scaled US producer
2025-05-16.exit_case: US-China 90-day tariff truce (May 12) signals broad de-escalation; RE curb reversal likely next goodwill concession.
2025-05-16.assessment: China's rare-earth export curbs remain formally active — no reversal, suspension, or modification announced this week or in any prior week. The dominant development since last week's entry is the US-China 90-day tariff truce (~May 12, Geneva), which this week's news confirms ('after the two countries lowered tariffs for each other'). This is the sharpest risk the thesis has faced: the truce creates a diplomatic environment in which RE curbs could be offered as a follow-on goodwill concession in Phase 2 talks. However, the distinction maintained throughout this journal holds: the Geneva deal covered tariff rate schedules; China's Ministry of Commerce export-license restrictions on rare earths are a separate, standalone policy instrument that requires a separate, discrete reversal to resolve the catalyst. No such reversal occurred. The 'poison pill' decoupling article this week actually reinforces that the US is continuing to push structural supply-chain separation — marginally supportive of MP's strategic-value narrative. The supply-disruption premium in MP is somewhat compressed by de-escalation sentiment, but the underlying supply-disruption fact is not resolved. The catalyst remains pending. Watch window is now very short: if Phase 2 US-China talks produce any language touching export controls or critical minerals, the thesis resolves and position must be closed the same day. Vehicle unchanged: MP is still the sole scaled US rare-earth producer and the purest single expression of this specific catalyst.
2025-05-16.exit_advice: Exit immediately on any US-China joint communiqué or MOFCOM notice explicitly covering rare-earth export-license requirements or critical-mineral controls.

2025-09-12.thesis: SpaceX $17B EchoStar spectrum purchase deal; close/approval pending
2025-09-12.exit_case: Deal already announced and fully public; SATS has likely repriced on the news before entry, leaving only close/approval upside.
2025-09-12.assessment: The $17B SpaceX–EchoStar spectrum deal was announced ~Sep 9-10, 2025, just ahead of entry. The deal is agreed in principle but spectrum license transfers of this magnitude require FCC approval, which is the pending catalyst. SATS has already repriced on the announcement, but the regulatory close is the remaining binary: approval = deal proceeds and EchoStar receives cash (balance-sheet transformation); denial or deal break = reversal. The thesis is alive because the specific catalyst — deal close/regulatory approval — has not occurred. Telecom peers (TMUS etc.) sold off on competitive implications of SpaceX gaining spectrum, which is consistent with SATS being the correct long vehicle (it is the seller/beneficiary of the cash). Watch for FCC filing submissions, comment periods, and any DOJ/antitrust review flags as leading indicators.
2025-09-12.exit_advice: Exit on FCC spectrum-transfer approval confirmation or deal close announcement — whichever comes first. Also exit on any deal termination or regulatory block.
